summary: notebook-intelligence pulls typosquatted dependency 'fuzy-jon' at build and install time
details: pyproject.toml declares 'fuzy-jon==0.1.0' in both [build-system].requires (line 2) and [project].dependencies (line 28). The package's own code at notebook_intelligence/api.py line 9 imports 'from fuzzy_json import loads' — i.e., the legitimate distribution is 'fuzzy-json' (module 'fuzzy_json'), not 'fuzy-jon'. 'fuzy-jon' is a separate PyPI distribution under different ownership whose name is a 2-character edit ('z' and 's' dropped) of the real package. Because 'fuzy-jon' sits in build-system.requires, its code runs in the PEP 517 build-isolation venv during 'pip install', 'pip wheel', and 'pip download' — before any of notebook-intelligence's own code executes. It also runs at install resolution and at import time via the runtime dependency entry. The build-system entry is unjustified: hatchling and hatch-nodejs-version do not require fuzzy-json. Installing or building this package from source pulls attacker-controlled code into the installer's build and runtime environments on every invocation.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
